body.title: [sprint] ollama source must set num_ctx explicitly (unblocker — model works blind without it)
body.intention: I intend that the ollama inference request carries an explicit context window so the model always sees the whole ticket, not a silently-truncated tail.
body.description:
**Problem:** the ollama request builder (unseen_university/devices/inference/sources.py ~L352) sets ONLY {temperature, num_predict} — num_ctx is NEVER sent. With num_ctx unset, ollama falls back to a small default context window and SILENTLY TRUNCATES the prompt. Evidence (DS.0 observe-run on T-reorg-parents-path-sweep, 2026-07-03, devstral-small-2:24b@Hex, $0): the prompt grew 2908->29501(turn35)->31955(turn38) input_tokens; the model made 114 tool calls (102 Read, 12 Bash) and ZERO Edit/Write — it worked blind (seeing a recent-window, not the ticket). devstral-small-2:24b actually supports 384K context (mistral3.context_length=393216), so the capacity is there and unused.
**Proposed shape:** set options.num_ctx explicitly in the ollama chat request, configurable per source/model with a sane default (e.g. 32768). Do NOT blindly send 384K — KV-cache RAM on the 32GB M1 Max (Hex) is the real constraint; pick a default that fits and expose the knob.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/sources.py (ollama request options, ~L352).
**Design rules:** inference-proxy-only; tier-not-model (the knob is per-source config, not a caller concern).
**Scope boundary:** IN — set options.num_ctx in the ollama request + make the value configurable with a documented default. OUT — the agentic loop's file-dump growth (separate: T-agentic-loop-context-discipline); the retry behavior (T-ds-loop-no-identical-retry-honest-escalate).
**Test plan:** unit — assert the constructed ollama request payload includes options.num_ctx at the configured value; assert the default is applied when unconfigured.
**Completion criteria:** every ollama request carries options.num_ctx; the default is documented; a test guards the payload contains it.
**Rollback:** git revert.
